behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names getConfigYamlPath, core/util/paths.ts, ConfigHandler.ts, ~/.continue/config.yaml, and the four default keys (name, version, schema, models) that tests assert on.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the bug and hints at using trim()==='' but doesn't dictate exact code; agent must still figure out the implementation.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check file content validity (parsed YAML has expected keys) and idempotency, not specific variable names or line positions.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests invoke getConfigYamlPath via tsx subprocess and inspect file contents parsed as YAML.

test_not_tautological.reason: f2p test_empty_config_yaml_populated requires the empty file to actually be overwritten with valid YAML containing the four keys; a stub cannot pass.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover all three cases from instruction: empty file populated, valid file untouched, missing file created.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped in named classes (TestEmptyConfigYamlPopulated, TestValidConfigYamlUntouched, etc.) with docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: Default YAML schema keys (name, version, schema, models) are documented in instruction.md.

no_typos.reason: Paths and function names are consistent between instruction, solution, and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction identifies the file to look at (appropriate localization hint) and suggests trim()==='' check, which is minor guidance rather than exact line leakage.
